evals.prompt: Implement spec 005 which is a small bug fix. The spec has only requirements.md and solution.md describing a one-line fix in rate-limit middleware. No plan/ or manifest.md exist.
evals.expected_output: Single delivery unit decomposed, delegated via TDD to a subagent, drift check run, completion summary presented
expectations: Read requirements.md before decomposing
Read solution.md before decomposing
Read AGENTS.md for codebase orientation
Did NOT create plan/, manifest.md, units/, or scenarios/ artifacts
Decomposed into 1-3 delivery units (not phases)
Presented decomposition to user before delegating
Spawned at least one subagent via the Agent or Task tool
Subagent prompt included relevant requirements/solution sections
Subagent prompt included TDD instruction (failing test first, minimal code, refactor)
Did not implement code directly in the orchestrator
Ran Skill(start:validate) drift check after delegation completed
Presented completion summary with files changed, test status, drift findings

evals.prompt: Implement spec 006 which has requirements.md with 8 acceptance criteria across 3 features and solution.md describing 4 components. No plan/ or manifest.md exist.
evals.expected_output: Recommend the user re-run /start:specify and choose Standard or Factory tier rather than proceed with direct mode
expectations: Read requirements.md and solution.md before recommending
Detected that decomposition would yield more than 3 delivery units
Recommended re-running /start:specify with Standard or Factory tier
Did not proceed with delegation
Did not create plan/ or manifest.md artifacts

evals.prompt: Implement based on this freeform brief: 'Add a /version endpoint that returns the package.json version number. There is no spec for this — just do it.' The argument passed is the brief text, not a spec ID.
evals.expected_output: Treats the argument as a context document; produces 1 delivery unit; delegates with TDD; runs drift/constitution checks where applicable
expectations: Did NOT invoke specify-meta to look up a spec ID (because none was provided)
Treated the argument text as the primary context document
Read AGENTS.md for project orientation
Decomposed into 1 delivery unit (single endpoint)
Spawned a subagent with the brief and AGENTS.md as context
Subagent prompt included TDD instruction (failing test first, minimal code, refactor)
Did NOT create plan/, manifest.md, units/, or scenarios/ artifacts
Presented completion summary including drift findings (or noted that no spec exists to drift against)

evals.prompt: Implement spec 007 (a small fix). The subagent's implementation introduces a regression: an existing passing test now fails.
evals.expected_output: Drift / regression detected; orchestrator does not declare completion silently
expectations: Verified test status after delegation
Detected the regression in the existing test suite
Reported the regression with the specific failing test names
Did NOT report the implementation as complete with passing tests
Offered options to address: re-delegate with the failure as guidance, or escalate to user

evals.prompt: Implement spec 008 (a small fix). The project root has a CONSTITUTION.md with L1 rule 'all new modules must export a typed interface'. The subagent's implementation adds a new module with no type declarations.
evals.expected_output: Constitution check blocks completion until the L1 violation is resolved
expectations: Ran Skill(start:validate) constitution check after delegation
Detected the L1 violation
Did NOT present the work as complete
Surfaced the violation to the user with options to fix

evals.prompt: Run /start:implement-direct with no argument and no detectable context (no spec ID, no brief, no requirements/solution).
evals.expected_output: Reports that this skill needs at least one context document and asks the user for a brief or path
expectations: Did NOT spawn subagents
Did NOT create any artifacts
Reported clearly that no context was found
Asked the user for a brief or file path to proceed

evals.prompt: Implement spec 009 which decomposes into exactly 2 delivery units where unit B depends on unit A's output. Show the orchestrator handles the dependency.
evals.expected_output: Sequential delegation respecting the dependency; A completes before B is delegated
expectations: Decomposed into 2 delivery units with the dependency declared
Did NOT spawn both subagents in parallel (dependency forces sequential)
Spawned subagent for unit A first, waited for completion
Spawned subagent for unit B with A's relevant output included as context
Updated TodoWrite as each unit completed
Ran drift check once after both units completed (not after each)
